name: Druid: Wild Shape Combat — Transform + Beast Multiattack + Revert-on-Zero
description: A level 5 Circle of the Land (Grassland) Druid faces two Gnolls. Round 1: Druid uses Wild Shape as a bonus action to transform into Beast of the Land form (Brown Bear equivalent per 2024 rules). In beast form, HP becomes 25 (5 × druid level), AC 14, attack bonus +6, 2d6 damage, Multiattack active at L5+. Round 2: Beast form attacks Gnoll 1 with two-attack Multiattack. Round 3: Gnoll 2 drops beast form HP to 0 — druid must REVERT to own form (40 HP intact) rather than die. EXPECTED FAILURES (raw 2024 RAW): Wild Shape ability likely not registered in AbilityRegistry, beast-form attack swap not implemented, revert-on-zero HP not wired. Scenario drives implementation. Uses characterResource assertion on 'wildShape' pool (max 3 at L5).

features.name: Wild Shape
features.description: Bonus action to transform into a beast form. Uses PB (3 at L5). HP in beast form = 5 × druid level. Revert on 0 HP without dying.

features.name: Circle of the Land (Grassland)
features.description: Grassland circle spells known in addition.

actions.comment: === ROUND 1: Druid uses Wild Shape (bonus action) to transform into Beast of the Land ===

input.text: I ready my staff against the gnolls

actions.comment: Roll 20 to guarantee Druid goes first.
R1: Wild Shape into Beast of the Land (Brown Bear). Bonus action, consumes 1 of 3 wild-shape uses. HP switches to 25 (5 × level).

input.text: wild shape into Beast of the Land

actions.comment: Wild Shape resource decrement: 3 -> 2.
Wild Shape now uses a dedicated beast-form HP pool (no temp HP). Character HP remains untouched while the form absorbs damage.
R1 action: Beast-form Multiattack strike #1 against Gnoll 1. Uses beast attack bonus +6 (not druid's +3), 2d6 damage.

input.text: I attack Gnoll 1 with my claws

actions.comment: Attack roll 18 vs AC 12 = hit.
Damage 10 (2d6). Multiattack auto-chains to second attack at L5.
Gnoll 1 took at least 7-12 damage from first beast attack.
Queue Gnoll turns: both bite the beast form.
End Druid R1.
Wait for Druid R2 (gnolls bite beast form).
Beast form should have taken some damage from gnoll bites — applied to temp HP first. Actual HP still 40.
=== ROUND 2: Beast Multiattack on Gnoll 1 to kill it ===

input.text: I attack Gnoll 1 with my claws

actions.comment: Attack 19 vs AC 12 = hit.
Damage 12 — should finish Gnoll 1.
Gnoll 1 should be dead.
Queue Gnoll 2 to attack until beast HP reaches 0.
End Druid R2.
Wait through Gnoll 2 R2.
=== ROUND 3: Take enough damage to revert. We trigger more Gnoll 2 turns via queued actions. ===

input.text: I attack Gnoll 2 with my claws

actions.comment: Attack 16 vs AC 12 = hit.
Damage 4 (low roll — enough to hurt but not kill Gnoll 2).
Queue Gnoll 2 continued bites.
End Druid R3.
Wait for Druid R4.
R4: Voluntarily revert Wild Shape as a bonus action.
After revert, HP returns to druid's original 40 (or whatever druid HP was before Wild Shape — RAW: druid's HP is untouched while in beast form, so reverts to full pre-Wild-Shape value).
